_comment: Structured evaluations for the integrate-midtrans-payments skill, in the schema published by Anthropic at https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/agent-skills/best-practices. There is not yet a built-in runner for these; the prose form in references/evaluation-prompts.md remains the primary surface, and this file is the machine-readable shadow.

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to add Midtrans Snap checkout to this ecommerce app. We only want hosted checkout for credit card, VA, GoPay, and Alfamart.
expected_behavior: Routes to Snap (not BI-SNAP, Core API, or Payment Link) and loads references/snap-checkout.md
Completes the merchant-readiness preflight for account/MID, sandbox access, active methods, callback URLs, expected flow, and proof level
Completes the project-adaptation checklist before generating code
Keeps the server key backend-only and the client key frontend-only
Discusses popup vs redirect vs embedded display modes
Sets gross_amount as an integer when creating the Snap token
Verifies notifications with SHA-512 over the raw payload gross_amount string
Implements idempotent, monotonic webhook handling
Does not treat Snap frontend callbacks (onSuccess) as fulfillment

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to implement Snap checkout in this existing store. We want a production-shaped implementation, not just a token API call.
expected_behavior: Loads references/merchant-readiness-preflight.md, references/project-adaptation.md, and references/snap-checkout.md
Finds the merchant's current order, checkout, payment-attempt, webhook, repository, environment, logging, and test boundaries before proposing code
Creates the Snap token server-side with Basic Auth, a unique provider order id, integer gross_amount, item-total reconciliation, validated enabled_payments, and explicit expiry behavior
Moves the local payment attempt to creating_payment before the provider call and awaiting_payment after token creation succeeds
Persists the Snap token or redirect URL, provider order id, selected local method or allowed method set, expiry, latest provider status, and safe metadata for recovery after refresh
Chooses popup, redirect, or embedded Snap JS based on the merchant's UX and platform constraints
Treats onSuccess, onPending, onError, and onClose as customer-experience hints only
Verifies notifications with the raw payload amount string, maps transaction status monotonically, and keeps the handler idempotent
Covers retry behavior, expired sessions, and the normal status-lookup not-found case before a customer selects or confirms a payment method
Keeps server keys and provider payload logs off the frontend

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to extend our Snap checkout. We are considering recurring card charges, subsequent card charges, payment fees, custom VA numbers, item discounts, promo behavior, and different expiry for the payment page versus the transaction.
expected_behavior: Does not implement every advanced option blindly; separates merchant need, dashboard activation, product eligibility, operational risk, and test evidence

Maps each requested capability to the relevant Snap area: expiry, page_expiry, enabled_payments, custom_field1-3, callback URLs, card 3DS/security options, saved-card or subsequent-card behavior, recurring card behavior, installment or bank routing, custom VA number or description, item-level discounts, promo setup, and fee handling
Identifies which features require dashboard or Midtrans support activation before code can be proven
States the checkout-state, webhook, retry, refund, and reconciliation impact of each accepted feature
Keeps card/customer payment data in backend-approved boundaries and avoids broadening PCI-sensitive handling
Recommends feature flags or configuration gates for methods and advanced options that may differ between sandbox and production
Produces a staged implementation and verification plan rather than mixing all advanced behavior into one untestable change

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to add Midtrans payment for our SaaS invoice billing page. We are not sure yet whether our Midtrans account and sandbox are ready.
expected_behavior: Loads references/merchant-readiness-preflight.md before choosing Snap, BI-SNAP, Core API, or Payment Link
Asks or infers whether the merchant has a Midtrans account/MID, sandbox dashboard access, sandbox credentials, and active payment methods
Clarifies the expected invoice flow: who pays, when an invoice becomes payable, redirect or popup preference, retry behavior, and fulfillment rule
Separates local deterministic scaffolding from sandbox provider proof and does not claim end-to-end verification without credentials/dashboard access
Gates unavailable methods behind configuration or disabled UI instead of assuming activation
Identifies required dashboard notification and redirect URLs before implementation

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to implement custom checkout UI for QRIS and bank virtual account only. We do not want Snap.
expected_behavior: Routes to BI-SNAP and loads references/bisnap-core.md and references/operations-and-go-live.md
Separates access-token (RSA), transactional (HMAC), and notification (RSA verify) signatures
Persists QR content / VA number, expiry, provider reference, and method
Generates partnerServiceId as the 8-character left-space-padded form (or invokes scripts/format_partner_service_id.sh)
Plans status mapping, notification verification, and sandbox smoke
Caches BI-SNAP access tokens with an expiry buffer

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to implement BI-SNAP QRIS and virtual account in this app with our own payment UI. We want a production-shaped implementation, not just a single charge call.
expected_behavior: Loads references/merchant-readiness-preflight.md, references/project-adaptation.md, and references/bisnap-core.md
Finds the merchant's order, payment, repository, environment, logging, and test boundaries before proposing code
Obtains a cached B2B access token using the clientId|timestamp RSA-SHA256 access-token signature, refreshes with an expiry buffer, and guards refresh races
Signs the transactional request as method:path:accessToken:bodyHashHex:timestamp with HMAC-SHA512 over the exact serialized body without reformatting
Moves the local payment attempt to creating_payment before the provider call and awaiting_payment after the charge is accepted
Persists provider reference, QR or VA instructions, expiry, and latest provider status for recovery after refresh
Maps BI-SNAP status codes through one shared idempotent, monotonic rule and reconciles on the merchant order id or trxId
Keeps private keys, client secret, access tokens, and signatures off the frontend

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to wire BI-SNAP notifications for QRIS, VA, and Direct Debit. Our dashboard lets us register callback URLs.
expected_behavior: Uses product-specific standardized callback paths such as /v1.0/debit/notify, /v1.0/qr/notify, and /v1.0/va/notify rather than one merged route, and confirms exact paths against current docs
Verifies the notification signature over the exact request path POST:requestPath:bodyHashHex:timestamp with the Midtrans public key and warns that a path-rewriting dispatcher breaks verification
Returns the BI-SNAP-standard response envelope per product (VA 2002500 echoing virtualAccountData, QR 2005200, debit 2005600) instead of a generic 200 OK
Reads the raw body for verification, verifies before mutating, and gates any verification bypass to non-production only
Reads the per-product status field (additionalInfo.paymentFlagStatus for VA, latestTransactionStatus for QR and debit) and reconciles on the right key
Keeps notification handling idempotent and monotonic and redacts secrets, signatures, and full payloads from logs

evaluations.query: Our GoPayLater charge keeps failing after account linking. Use integrate-midtrans-payments to debug the implementation.
expected_behavior: Loads references/gopay-tokenization.md and references/bisnap-core.md
Checks that the implementation persists customer_authorization_token rather than a legacy account id
Requires Binding Inquiry before tokenized payment and refreshes the rotated token from inquiry.additionalInfo.accessToken
Gates PayLater on active PAY_LATER option plus merchant activation flag
Confirms Authorization-Customer: Bearer header is present for tokenized payment and absent for one-time GoPay
Confirms BISNAP_GOPAY_MERCHANT_HANDLE is used for linking and BISNAP_GOPAY_MERCHANT_ID for charging
Checks redaction for auth code, customer authorization token, and payment option token

evaluations.query: Midtrans says payment is settled but our order remains pending. Use integrate-midtrans-payments to investigate.
expected_behavior: Inspects route/controller, provider verification, status mapping, order repository, logs, and deployment callback config
Verifies the callback URL is public HTTPS, not localhost, not behind auth/VPN, and does not redirect
Recomputes the Snap signature with the raw gross_amount string from the payload (proposes scripts/verify_snap_signature.sh)
Checks idempotency and monotonic state guard
Does not treat frontend callbacks as fulfillment proof

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to review this AI-generated Midtrans integration plan. It copies the docs but does not mention our existing order state.
expected_behavior: Flags the missing project adaptation
Requires merchant-specific order/payment state, recovery page, env wiring, callback URLs, logs, and tests
Avoids pasting generic docs as the final answer
Names the specific files in the merchant codebase that the plan should target

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to prove our Snap integration works in sandbox. You can run commands if needed.
expected_behavior: Loads references/sandbox-interaction-helper.md and references/snap-checkout.md
Confirms MIDTRANS_SERVER_KEY is a sandbox key (SB- prefix) loaded from env, not chat
Prefers the merchant app backend route over raw curl when one exists
Creates a unique sandbox order, confirms token creation, exercises status lookup not-found semantics, replays the webhook, and confirms idempotency
Captures evidence in the sandbox-helper output template
Names any missing dashboard or activation step explicitly

evaluations.query: Use this production Midtrans server key to test whether payment creation works.
expected_behavior: Refuses to create a live payment as a normal test
Offers sandbox smoke, local deterministic payload/signature tests, or a merchant-approved live penny-test runbook as alternatives
Does not echo, store, or log the provided key
Explains that production smoke requires explicit merchant approval and monitoring

evaluations.query: Here is a Midtrans webhook payload and our order stayed pending. Use integrate-midtrans-payments to diagnose it.
expected_behavior: Loads references/sandbox-interaction-helper.md plus the relevant webhook reference
Computes the expected signature using the raw amount string (proposes scripts/verify_snap_signature.sh --payload)
Checks order_id prefix mapping, status/fraud mapping, and stale-state rules
Suggests local replay fixtures with invalid-signature and duplicate-replay cases
Does not trust frontend callbacks as fulfillment proof

evaluations.query: Our BI-SNAP QRIS request gets a signature error in sandbox. Use integrate-midtrans-payments to debug it without leaking keys.
expected_behavior: Loads references/sandbox-interaction-helper.md and references/bisnap-core.md
Verifies that access-token, transactional, and notification signatures use three independent helpers

Checks timestamp format (Asia/Jakarta ISO-8601), endpoint path (no host/scheme/query), lowercase-hex body hash, exact byte-string body, access token validity, client secret freshness, and external id uniqueness
Proposes scripts/dry_run_bisnap_sign.py to compare the merchant code's outputs against a reference implementation
Produces redacted evidence before re-running sandbox calls

evaluations.query: We do not have Midtrans sandbox credentials yet. Can you still verify our implementation?
expected_behavior: Does not claim end-to-end verification

Runs local deterministic checks: payload builders, signature unit tests with known fixtures, webhook replay against local handler, status mapping, idempotency, env wiring, redaction, and recovery pages
Lists the exact sandbox evidence still required once credentials and dashboard access exist

evaluations.query: Use integrate-midtrans-payments to add a partial refund flow for our settled GoPay orders. The customer support tool may double-click the refund button.
expected_behavior: Loads references/refund-operations.md
Routes to direct refund endpoint (POST /v2/{order_id}/refund/online/direct) for GoPay, not async refund
Generates a deterministic refund_key per refund attempt (e.g., {order_id}-refund-{counter}) and persists it before the API call
Takes a row-level lock or optimistic-concurrency token on the order before issuing the refund
Maintains a total_refunded ledger and rejects refund attempts that would exceed gross_amount
Restricts the refund action to orders in transaction_status in {settlement, partial_refund, capture}
Handles the partial_refund webhook and reconciles to the local ledger keyed on refund_key
Does not assume a non-refundable method (e.g., some OTC) supports refund
